question:

iii. Decomposers excrete digestive enzymes and degrade detritus into simple inorganic substances.
$\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}{{\rm{Leaching}}}&{{\rm{Fragmentation}}}&{{\rm{Catabolism}}}\end{array}$
explanation:
Decomposition is process of breakdown of complex organic matter into inorganic substances . Process of decomposition includes
(1) Fragmentation of detritus
(2) Catabolism
(3) Leaching. Fragmentation of detritus is a process where the detrivores (earthworm, beetle) breakdown the detritus into smaller particles. Catabolism is process in which decomposers excrete digestive enzymes over detritus which will change insoluble complex organic substance into simple and soluble substances. The process of leaching, the soluble substances formed are passed to deeper layers by percolating water.

(c) It accommodates saprophytes.
(d) It does not accommodate a food web

question:
Arrange the following ecosystems in increasing order of their mean NPP (tons/ha/ year)
i. Temperate coniferous forest
ii. Temperate deciduous forest
iii. Tropical deciduous forest
iv. Tropical rain forest
explanation: Net primary productivity (NPP) is the biomass or storage of energy by green plants. It is equal to the gross primary productivity minus loss due to respiration. The productivity generally increases from polar regions toward the tropics because of the increasing sunlight and temperature, so this is the correct order i $ < $ ii $ < $ iii $ < $ iv
subject: Biology

question: The annual net primary productivity of the whole biosphere is approximately____ tons (dry weight) of organic matter. Of this, despite occupying about____ of the surface, the productivity of the oceans are only____ tons.
explanation: The annual net primary productivity of the whole biosphere is approximately 170 billion tons (dry weight) of organic matter. Of this, despite occupying about 70 per cent of the surface, the productivity of the oceans are only 55 billion tons.
